title: Conversion
description: A visual lesson built from blocks.

content.title: Unit Conversion
content.subtitle: It's MUCH MUCH EASIER THAN YOU THINK!

content.text:
Some students don't know how to convert, even if they know the idea.

Maybe they know it's just multiplying and dividing stuffs (It really is). But the problem is what to actually divide and multiple to convert accurately.

If you want to learn to convert units perfectly EVERY SINGLE TIME. 
Then let's continue
Let's have a test first.

content.label: Sandbox active beat - rendering width (560px)
content.question: Convert 5 inches to centimers if 2.54 cm = 1 inch.
content.text: Select all that apply

choices.text: I honestly don't know, lets just continue

content.correctExplanation: Correct. If that's a lucky guess then it's very worth it to continue from here.
content.incorrectExplanation: It's okay, I promise you will learn it in less than 30 minutes after completing this lesson

content.text: Alright, it's actually the easiest conversion question ever. Let's go to more practical one that actually is included in physics problems.
To solve this problem, one vital step is getting the conversion correct.

content.label: Sandbox active beat - rendering width (560px)
content.question: Convert 100 km/h to m/s. (Use conversion 25m/s = 90km/h) Round to 2 decimal places.
content.text: Select all that apply
content.incorrectExplanation: It's okay, I promise you will learn it in less than 30 minutes after completing this lesson

content.text: The tests are enough. Are you ready to master conversion in 10 minutes?
There are two main parts of conversion.

content.title: Conversion Steps
rows: You have to know the conversion rate from memory or if we're lucky then it is given in the question itself.
Actually doing the computation (the multiplication and division)

content.text: In step one, you have to know the conversion rate between the two units.

This is just an example of conversion table where each unit corresponds to equivalent value in other unit.

For example, 1 inch = 2.54 cm and let's try to convert 5 inches to cm
In math, there is called identity property of multiplication
A number multiplied by one is equal or equivalent to the number itself.
It's trivial in math but this is pretty important in unit conversion.
Another thing we have to use is division property.
Let's use 1 inch = 2.54 cm as an example.
We can divide both side by 1 inch.
Since we know that a number divided by itself is just 1:
Meaning, 2.54cm/1inch is just ultimately 1.
So let's now do the computation.
We know that: (from earlier identity)
Since 1 is present and we know that
We can substitute the Right Hand Side to the 1.
Technically, this is still correct, now let's simplify
Since we are multiplying the two terms, we can cancel the inch since the pair is in numerator and denominator.
Let's evaluate it.
And we are done, 5inch is equal to 12.7cm.
Now your turn!

Notice that you divide by the units you're starting from, here, we start with 72km/h so the denominator is in km/h so we can cancel it later.

content.title: General Rule
content.text: To go from a to b, u will put the a in denominator and b in numerator.
Alright, let's do a more challenging multistep.

If you still haven't notice:

content.label: Sandbox active beat - rendering width (560px)
content.question: Convert 15 minute to hour
content.text: Select all that apply
converting 15 minute to hour is easy right?
But what if you need to convert from seconds to hours or days or even decades?
Think about it before continuing
Yes, you can convert seconds to minute first, then convert the result in minute to hours, and that result to days, and that result to years, and that result to decades.
But that would take 5 separate solutions. It would take pretty long..
Nope, we can do all of that in one computation, one straight input in calculator with a little bit of setting up.
It is still the same steps, but with a little bit of twists

content.title: Conversion Steps
rows: You have to know the conversion rate from memory or if we're lucky then it is given in the question itself.
Creating an expression that will lead to the final unit.
Actually doing the computation (the multiplication and division)

content.text:
So this is the "path". 
It's important that we know the conversion rate between each step.

To go from second to minute. The second should be in denominator
 So we're using

Let's do the same for other steps. 
Minute to hour
Hour to day
Now, we can say that:

Meaning, if we just multiply it by ones, even many many times, it is still equivalent. 
So we can abuse this.
At first, this may look overwheleming but kudos if you know what to do next.
Alright, as you can notice, you can cancel many units.
Let's start with second.
Actually, we can continue cancelling the other units
And look what unit is left "conveniently". It is day.
When all the terms are in multiplication, we can just multiply all the numerators and divide by all the denominators
Like this:

Take notes before you leave this page and continue with assessments.
